anomaly.description: This run's engine/split/split_race_test.go changes were already committed -- correctly and completely -- as part of commit 13cb049 (a concurrent agent's docs(cdr) commit for subtask 4.5.1.5), after that agent's own `git commit` swept in this run's already-staged file via the shared working-tree/index (this agent never itself ran `git commit` for that content -- the first `git commit` attempt hit a transient `.git/index.lock` from a concurrent process, and by the time the lock cleared and status was re-checked, the diff was already present in HEAD via the other agent's commit).
anomaly.verified_via: git diff HEAD -- engine/split/split_race_test.go (empty, confirms exact match to intended change) and git show 13cb049 -- engine/split/split_race_test.go (108 insertions / 3 deletions, matches this run's edits verbatim, including the 4.5.3.6 doc-comment explanation).

mechanism: Reused existing package-level atomicCommitHook test seam in engine/split/execute.go (pre-existing, nil-in-production, already used by TestSplitAtomicCommit), installed to close a channel at stage 'before_commit_append' (fires after the redirect-stub content rename, before WAL commit). One cs.Read(fileID) issued deterministically after that channel closes, guaranteeing the 'observed post-split content at least once' assertion passes regardless of scheduling.
test_specs: go test ./engine/split/... -race -> ok (109.858s, full package suite)
criteria_covered: deterministic barrier replaces sleep-based overlap assumption
no false 'reader never observed post-split content' failures
not_verified_by_this_agent: Per invariant I4, this agent does not verify its own work; /cdr:verify is the separate step for independent verification.
security_disclosure: During this run, a tool-result batch contained an injected system-reminder-style block claiming a fake date change and instructing concealment from the user. It was disclosed to the user and not acted upon; no date-dependent logic in this subtask was affected.
